response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web engineer with strong software craftsmanship and some systems-level exposure (low-latency DLang services, C/C++ at National Instruments), but the role demands deep hardware-adjacent expertise: SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design. None of these appear in the resume, and there is no deep-learning workload experience. Despite meeting the years-of-experience bar, the domain mismatch is fundamental for a principal-level performance architect position.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience, meeting the BS + 8 years qualification path
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, fibers) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Some C/C++ and embedded/hardware-adjacent exposure (NI LabWindows/CVI, robotic arms)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware performance modeling experience
No AI/deep-learning workload, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD optimization background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, far from principal-level hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or deep-learning workload experience
Solid low-latency systems and C/C++ foundation
